theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Materials (+1.17%) and Communication Svcs (+0.84%) led the market today, while Health Care (-1.05%) and Energy (-0.42%) brought up the rear. Performance didn't cluster cleanly by style — growth averaged +0.55%, defensives -0.07%, and value/cyclicals +0.14% — more a stock-by-stock market than a single broad theme. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.34%, Dow +0.29%, Nasdaq +0.27% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -0.31%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX fell 3.16% to 15.34, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold slipped 0.47% to $4,110.00, crude oil was little changed at $71.16, and bitcoin gained 1.12% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
